name: Bathroom Renovation Planner
description: Plan your bathroom renovation from start to finish. Estimate material quantities, compare finish options, build a planning budget, and generate your shopping list.
metaDescription: Free bathroom renovation planner. Compare tile, vinyl, and microcement finishes. Estimate budget, timeline, and materials. Interactive planning tool.

scopeTiers.name: Cosmetic Refresh
scopeTiers.description: Paint, new fixtures, updated hardware, and lighting. Keep existing layout and plumbing.

scopeTiers.name: Mid-Range Upgrade
scopeTiers.description: New floor and wall tile, updated vanity, refreshed tub or shower surround. Minor layout changes.

scopeTiers.name: Full Gut Renovation
scopeTiers.description: Strip to studs. New plumbing, electrical, and ventilation. Custom tile work and premium fixtures.
includes: Strip to studs, remove all fixtures

qualityTiers.name: Budget-Friendly
qualityTiers.description: Practical materials that perform well at accessible price points.

qualityTiers.name: Mid-Range
qualityTiers.description: Quality materials with good durability and style. Best value overall.

qualityTiers.name: Premium
qualityTiers.description: Designer materials, custom tile, and high-end fixtures. Maximum quality and durability.

options.name: Ceramic Tile
options.description: Classic glazed ceramic. Waterproof, durable, endless style options.

options.name: Porcelain Tile
options.description: Denser than ceramic. Better for high-moisture areas. Through-body color.

options.name: Luxury Vinyl Plank
options.description: 100% waterproof. Warm underfoot. Easy to install and maintain.

options.name: Natural Stone
options.description: Marble, travertine, or slate. Unique veining. Requires sealing.

options.name: Paint (satin/semi-gloss)
options.description: Cost-effective. Needs mold-resistant paint. Refresh every few years.

options.name: Ceramic Tile (wainscot/full)
options.description: Waterproof classic. Easy to clean. Timeless look.

options.name: Subway Tile
options.description: Classic 3x6 brick pattern. Versatile, affordable, timeless.

options.name: Microcement
options.description: Seamless, modern. Applied over existing tile. No grout lines.

options.name: Large-Format Porcelain Slab
options.description: Minimal grout lines. Modern aesthetic. Premium durability.

options.name: Laminate
options.description: Budget-friendly. Many patterns. Easy to install.

options.name: Quartz
options.description: Engineered stone. Non-porous. Consistent color. Most popular mid-range choice.

options.name: Granite
options.description: Natural stone. Unique veining. Needs annual sealing.

options.name: Marble
options.description: Luxury natural stone. Etches easily. High maintenance.

finishCategories.name: Tub / Shower Surround
finishCategories.description: Tub or shower wall surround material

options.name: Acrylic Surround
options.description: One-piece or panel surround. Affordable, waterproof, low maintenance.

options.name: Tile Surround
options.description: Ceramic or porcelain tile on cement board. Customizable, durable.

options.name: Natural Stone Surround
options.description: Slate, travertine, or marble tile. Premium look. Needs sealing.

options.name: Stock Vanity
options.description: Pre-built standard sizes. Most affordable option.

options.name: Semi-Custom Vanity
options.description: Choose size, finish, and countertop. Better quality and fit.

options.name: Custom Vanity
options.description: Built to spec. Any size, any material. Premium hardware and soft-close drawers.

budgetCategories.name: Demolition & Disposal
budgetCategories.description: Removing old fixtures, tile, and debris. Dumpster or haul-away fees.

budgetCategories.name: Plumbing
budgetCategories.description: Rough-in work, new supply lines, drain pipes, and fixture installation.

budgetCategories.name: Electrical
budgetCategories.description: New wiring, outlets, switches, vanity lighting, and exhaust fan.

budgetCategories.name: Flooring & Subfloor
budgetCategories.description: Tile, underlayment, subfloor repair, and waterproof membrane.

budgetCategories.name: Walls & Ceiling
budgetCategories.description: Drywall, paint, tile walls, and trim work.

budgetCategories.name: Fixtures & Hardware
budgetCategories.description: Toilet, vanity, sink, faucet, tub/shower, mirrors, and accessories.

budgetCategories.name: Cabinetry & Countertop
budgetCategories.description: Vanity cabinet, countertop, backsplash, and storage.

budgetCategories.name: Ventilation
budgetCategories.description: Exhaust fan, ducting, and exterior vent.

budgetCategories.name: Permits & Inspections
budgetCategories.description: Building permits, inspection fees, and engineering if required.

budgetCategories.name: Contingency
budgetCategories.description: Unforeseen issues: water damage, outdated wiring, structural repairs.

timelinePhases.name: Planning & Design
timelinePhases.description: Design decisions, material selection, permits, and contractor quotes.

timelinePhases.name: Preparation
timelinePhases.description: Protect adjoining spaces, set up temporary arrangements, and prepare for demolition.
details: Protect floors and hallways with rosin paper
Turn off water and electricity to bathroom

timelinePhases.name: Demolition
timelinePhases.description: Remove old fixtures, tile, and finishes. Expose the rough structure.
details: Remove old tile from walls and floor
Remove drywall if doing full gut
Inspect for water damage or mold

timelinePhases.name: Rough-In Work
timelinePhases.description: Plumbing, electrical, and ventilation rough-in before walls are closed.
details: Run electrical wiring for lights, fan, outlets
Install blocking for grab bars and heavy fixtures
Install cement board or waterproof membrane in wet areas

timelinePhases.name: Walls & Ceiling
timelinePhases.description: Close walls, tape and mud drywall, prepare surfaces for tile and paint.
details: Install drywall on ceiling and non-wet walls
Tape, mud, and sand drywall joints
Install tile backer board in shower/tub area

timelinePhases.name: Flooring
timelinePhases.description: Prepare subfloor and install finished flooring.
details: Repair or level subfloor as needed
Install floor tile, vinyl, or other flooring
Protect finished floor with rosin paper

timelinePhases.name: Fixture Installation
timelinePhases.description: Install vanity, toilet, tub/shower, and all fixtures.

timelinePhases.name: Finishing Touches
timelinePhases.description: Trim, caulk, accessories, and final cleanup.
details: Caulk all joints (tile, counter, toilet, tub)
Install towel bars, toilet paper holder, robe hooks
Restore water and power, test everything

faqs.q: How long does a bathroom renovation take?
faqs.a: A cosmetic refresh can take 3-5 days. A mid-range renovation typically takes 2-3 weeks. A full gut renovation usually takes 4-6 weeks depending on scope, contractor availability, and material lead times.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for bathroom renovation?
faqs.a: Permit requirements vary by location. Generally, permits are required for structural changes, moving plumbing or electrical, and altering load-bearing walls. Cosmetic updates (paint, new fixtures in same location) often don't require permits. Check with your local building department.

faqs.q: Should I replace plumbing during a renovation?
faqs.a: If you have galvanized steel or polybutylene pipes, replacement is strongly recommended. For copper or PEX in good condition, replacement is optional. Access during a gut renovation makes pipe replacement far cheaper than doing it later.

faqs.q: What is the most expensive part of a bathroom renovation?
faqs.a: Plumbing and labor typically account for the largest share (30-40% combined). Custom tile work and premium fixtures are the next biggest cost drivers. On a per-square-foot basis, countertop and custom shower work tend to be the most expensive items.

faqs.q: Can I DIY a bathroom renovation?
faqs.a: Cosmetic updates (paint, new fixtures, replacing vanity) are DIY-friendly for experienced homeowners. Plumbing and electrical rough-in work should be done by licensed professionals in most jurisdictions. Full gut renovations with structural changes almost always require contractors.

faqs.q: How much contingency should I budget?
faqs.a: 15-20% of the total budget is standard for bathroom renovations. Common surprises include water damage behind walls, outdated wiring, mold remediation, and subfloor repairs. Opening walls during demolition is when most surprises are discovered.

faqs.q: What adds the most resale value to a bathroom renovation?
faqs.a: Mid-range renovations typically offer the best return on investment (60-70% cost recouped). Key value drivers: quality tile work, updated lighting, good ventilation, and neutral finish choices. Over-building for the neighborhood should be avoided.

faqs.q: Can I reuse existing cabinets in a bathroom renovation?
faqs.a: Existing cabinets can be reused if they are solid wood or plywood construction (not particle board) and in good condition. Refacing or repainting is an option. However, replacing usually costs little more than refacing and allows you to optimize the layout.
